reviews.review_description: I just defeated "Glassjaw Nelson" and it's been the most intense cardio workout in recent memory.

Collision detection for the "boxing lessons" is very bad. Throwing hooks with good form and hitting the target will result in an error noise (or nothing at all) rather than a connection. Lessons will randomly stop and skip to the next one, but then not allow you to move on.

No pass through on the version of Meta I have, so you basically have to stand still. Slipping isn't always detected. Sometimes punches "land" when you dodged them.

Foolishly, I tried this out because I liked the idea of its boxing technique minigames, and then decided to purchase it in a sale, hoping the problems would be less serious or fix themselves with an update. They didn't. Nor do the game's creators have any kind of easy access website that I can find to contact them.

So the overall experience is one of standing with your feet planted in a spot, trying to slip punches that hit you even after you slip them, and being stuck on a buggy boxing training mini game that you can't advance in, because hook collision detection doesn't work.

Buy Thrill of the Fight, or even one of the less popular boxing games before this one. The other major boxing sims don't have the problems this thing does.

EDIT: To be clear, the collision detection issues also exist in regular sparring. You have to slip and duck by such a wide margin to avoid the problem -- which doesn't always work even then -- that it's probably been reinforcing exaggerated, bad defensive body mechanics. It's not my headset, either, since it works just fine in Thrill of the Fight, Boxing Underdog, Ultraboxing...heck, even Creed. I recommend any of the first three mentioned as better alternatives for someone who actually wants to practice boxing skills. Mutant Boxing League looks nice, but some versions don't have pass-through (or at least mine doesn't) and the mechanics aren't there, for whatever reason.

developer_response.body: Hello, I am very sorry you did not like our game and got stuck on the boxing lessons. We will do our best to fix this, we have both a homepage and discord channel where you can post if you have more feedback than above? You can find the information on our Meta page. www.filmicstudios.se,  or Discord https://discord.gg/4qyJHAef

reviews.review_title: Great and well made boxing game
reviews.review_description: Fast start, (no exhausting intros) No motion sickness. Nice Arcade feeling to it, love the powerglove,  great gameplay for both beginners and experts. Also some fun minigames that I really appreciated.
Good game with fun oponents. you will sweat
Worse boxing game ever in from VR

Horrible
 Give me my money back

reviews.review_title: Fun game, but short lacks customization
reviews.review_description: Very fun game, but only around 1-2 hr of content. Compared to TOFT, it has better slipping/dodging,  and training mode. But it's lacking in movement without roomscale and tracking isn't as good, especially for hooks. Also would love some customization, e.g. disable arcade aspects, increase time per round, time between rounds, number of rounds, ...
A neat and fast exercise game with fun visuals and good soundtrack. Absolutely recommend to people looking for something they can quickly boot up and play.

reviews.review_title: Great game but it’s not a simulator
reviews.review_description:
The game is super fun, but if you’re looking for a simulator like TOTF, this is not it. This is a boxing-inspired game. For example, characters have powers and I don’t think punching harder/faster makes any difference, it’s more about placing your combos. There are also gloves that you unlock for extra power or speed.

One negative is that there’s no mixed reality for Quest 2, which is a huge issue for a boxing game (if I get fully immersed in the gameplay I’ll end up punching a wall). That limits my movement a lot.

The game seems well finished (which is very rare for VR titles), has nice graphics, no obvious bugs, good soundtrack, different opponents with different fight styles and skills, minigames and challenges that make it more fun,  great visuals  (opponents look very cool, decent AI, etc.

Again, it’s not a boxing simulator, but it is definitely fun and a great workout.

It's a really good game to teach you how to do the boxing. I have all the other boxing game,  but that's the only one trying to teach you.

After 30 mins playing, i also get sweaty. My heart beat is between 130 to 145, it's a great funny cardio game for me. Highly recommend.

Pin me, if you want to some discount

At first I thought it might be too easy, arcade game style, but it's a pretty good workout and the graphics are so much better than TOF. Best boxing game at the moment.

おすすめです👍

reviews.review_title: Super fun and a good workout too!
reviews.review_description: I love the graphics and the playability of this game. It can get super intense during a match so it’s also a good workout. They are some bugs here and there but totally worth checking out if you’re looking for a boxing type game.

reviews.review_title: Better than thrill of the fight
reviews.review_description: Awesome game, great graphics, mini games (worthy of a separate fitness app), better mechanics and overall nicer experience than in other boxing games.

This is really a well-polished (cartoony) punch-out-like title. Very fun and a good workout, maybe a bit less than Thrill (but also less injury-inducing). Crazy this doesn't immediately pop up when searching with the word "boxing"!

If you come from thrill, you will have no trouble bobbing and weaving around their punches. I am a little concerned it might be too easy and short. I hope there are ways to speed up the opponents and increase the required glove speed for me to do damage, etc to make the run-throughs harder (and me more fit!) EDIT: Found the difficulty settings. Plenty of fun to be had. 

The only question I have is if swinging harder helps score points or do more damage, or if we ar just supposed to touch them for fixed points. Maybe this could be made clearer in the tutorial.
A variety of ways to play.

The large set of mini games are fun challenges that test just how fast accurate & far you can go. The boxing is entertaining & a great workout. I highly recommend the game, as well the devs are very responsive on their discord.

The mechanics of the game are great! The boxing really feels good and I love the style of the game with these mutant characters. The music is also very good. The game didn't took me too long to 100%, but I'm still jumping in once in a while to get a good workout. I'm really sweaty after a 1 hour session haha. I hope that future update will have more challenges, or if they could implement a list of challenges to accomplish every week, that would be awesome!

Sorry, I don't understand all the great reviews. Graphics are crisp and clear, but your gloves look as big as pillows. The tutorial is very frustrating because no amount of recentering will get the training bot close enough to hit, and I didn't feel like it was any better in my first match. The price is very inflated. Maybe for $10 less.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback! We'll make sure to look into the issue. We have not heard anyone else having that problem. If you'd like to, it would be great if you could leave more detailed feedback in our discord: 
https://discord.gg/du39WgfyMU
Thanks!

reviews.review_description:
The game is not like Creed or Thrill of Fight. It plays more like a rhythm game, where you have to hit timed ques. This time though, its not to music, but to a boxing theme with mutants and mini games.

The game has a arcade, cartoony look to it, but don't let that fool you. This game can really workout you out. You will work the upper body doing punches, jab and hooks. The core will feel it from the crunches and twists and finally the lower body will feel it from doing squats.
After using this app, you can feel you have workout your muscles and cardio system. 

If you are looking for an arcade boxer like Creed, this is not it. If you are looking for a fun fitness app, that is not rhythm based, this is the app for you.

YouTube search IronbugVR for my video review.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! Just to clarify, this is not a rhythm game, the help indicators are only visible in the beginner mode as hints, and are not meant as timed cues (you are free to punch whenever you want). In response to your feedback, and to make it more clear, we have now updated the game so you also can disable these in the beginner mode. We appreciate the feedback!

reviews.review_description: Great, very enjoyable، I liked this game immediately without discussion after I tried the fun exercises. If you need a trainer and a boxing club, buy this fun game immediately.
